skill.name: literature-review
skill.description: Conduct systematic literature reviews across scientific and biomedical databases. The skill guides search strategy, screening, citation verification, synthesis, and PDF-ready review outputs.
skill.summary: Plan, screen, synthesize, verify, and format academic literature reviews.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but most markdown hits are false positives from fenced examples, DOI URLs, and academic syntax. Confirmed risks are legitimate but meaningful: Bash-enabled workflows, pandoc subprocess execution, DOI/Crossref network calls, and local file writes. No evidence found of credential theft, malicious obfuscation, destructive commands, or prompt injection attempts.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution for PDF Generation
medium_findings.description: The PDF helper executes pandoc and dependency checks through subprocess.run. Arguments are passed as lists without shell=True, which reduces injection risk, but the workflow still runs local external tools on user-provided files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are direct and confirmed. Risk is moderate because the commands are fixed tool invocations and do not use a shell.

medium_findings.title: Broad Bash Permission in Skill Metadata
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash as an allowed tool and instructs users to run helper scripts and package installers. This is expected for the workflow but increases execution risk compared with a documentation-only skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and command-oriented workflow are explicit. The commands appear legitimate, so this is an elevated operational risk, not confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Expected Network Calls to Citation Metadata Services
low_findings.description: The citation verifier sends DOI values to doi.org and Crossref to validate references. These are documented academic endpoints, but citation lists may reveal research interests to third parties.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network usage is clear and limited to citation validation. I found no evidence of credential collection or hidden exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Local File Reads and Writes for Review Outputs
low_findings.description: The helper scripts read user-provided review or result files and write formatted output reports. A user-selected output path could overwrite local files if used carelessly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are confirmed and match the documented output workflow. Risk is low because there is no hidden path construction beyond user input or a derived report filename.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown and Weak-Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged markdown backticks, hardcoded URLs, DOI examples, and bracket-heavy academic text are documentation patterns. I found no cryptographic algorithm implementation or obfuscated payload in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed citation examples, placeholders, and ordinary Python source. No suspicious encoding, crypto implementation, or executable markdown payload was present in the sampled static-hit context.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run Invokes External PDF Tools
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script invokes pandoc and xelatex-related dependency checks through subprocess.run.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The calls are present and security-relevant. They use list arguments and no shell, so injection risk is reduced.

dangerous_patterns.title: requests.Session Sends DOI Queries
dangerous_patterns.description: The citation verifier creates an HTTP session and queries DOI and Crossref services for metadata.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network destinations are explicit academic services. No evidence found of secret or file content exfiltration beyond DOI validation.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled Output File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The search processor writes formatted results to the output path supplied through command-line arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write path comes from user CLI input and is directly opened. This is normal CLI behavior but can overwrite files.

content.value_statement: Researchers need repeatable literature reviews with clear search methods and verified citations. This skill provides structured workflows, templates, helper scripts, and citation checks for academic review writing.
actual_capabilities: Guides systematic review planning with research questions, scope, and inclusion criteria.
Provides database search strategies for PubMed, bioRxiv, arXiv, Semantic Scholar, and related sources.
Processes exported search results with deduplication, ranking, filtering, and formatted output.
Verifies DOI references through DOI and Crossref metadata lookups.
Generates structured markdown reviews and PDF output using a bundled template and pandoc workflow.
limitations: It does not provide direct database credentials or guaranteed access to paywalled articles.
Citation verification requires network access to external DOI and metadata services.
PDF generation requires local pandoc and LaTeX dependencies.
Human review is still required for study screening, quality assessment, and final conclusions.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Systematic Review Protocol
use_cases.description: Define review questions, databases, search strings, eligibility criteria, and PRISMA documentation before screening starts.

use_cases.title: Synthesize Biomedical Evidence
use_cases.description: Aggregate PubMed, bioRxiv, and Semantic Scholar results into a structured thematic literature review.

use_cases.title: Clean and Verify References
use_cases.description: Deduplicate exported search results, check DOI metadata, and prepare consistent citations for submission.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Review Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a literature review on [topic]. Define the research question, scope, databases, keywords, and inclusion criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Build Search Strings
prompt_templates.prompt: Create reproducible search strings for PubMed, arXiv, and Semantic Scholar about [topic] from [start year] to [end year].

prompt_templates.title: Screen and Synthesize Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these exported results to deduplicate, rank, and organize papers by themes. Identify gaps and conflicts in the evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Finalize a Publication Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn my screened papers and notes into a PRISMA-aligned review with verified citations, limitations, quality assessment, and PDF-ready structure.

output_examples.input: Plan a review on CRISPR therapies for sickle cell disease from 2015 to 2026.
output: A scoped research question using the PICO framework.
Database-specific search strings for PubMed, bioRxiv, arXiv, and Semantic Scholar.
Eligibility criteria, screening stages, and a PRISMA documentation outline.

output_examples.input: Deduplicate and summarize my exported literature search results.
output: A cleaned list grouped by DOI or title matches.
A summary of counts by source, publication year, and citation impact.
A ranked markdown bibliography for screening.

output_examples.input: Verify citations in my final literature review draft.
output: A DOI verification summary with passed and failed references.
Corrected metadata suggestions from Crossref where available.
Formatted APA-style citations for verified references.

best_practices: Document every database, search date, query string, and result count for reproducibility.
Verify citations and metadata before final submission or PDF generation.
Use thematic synthesis and quality assessment instead of simple paper-by-paper summaries.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on a single database for comprehensive scientific coverage.
Do not include preprints without clearly labeling their review status.
Do not run helper scripts on sensitive unpublished drafts without considering external DOI lookups.

faq.question: Does this skill search databases directly?
faq.answer: It provides workflows and scripts for processing results. Database access depends on available tools, APIs, and user permissions.

faq.question: Can it create a systematic review?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports systematic review planning, search documentation, screening structure, synthesis, and PRISMA-style reporting.

faq.question: Does citation verification require internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. The verifier contacts DOI and Crossref services to check references and retrieve metadata.

faq.question: Can it generate a PDF?
faq.answer: Yes. PDF generation uses pandoc and LaTeX tools that must be installed locally.

faq.question: Is human review still needed?
faq.answer: Yes. Researchers must validate search choices, screening decisions, quality ratings, and scientific conclusions.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
